summary: Accepted next sprint plan: make builds high-signal transition contracts with mandatory feedback diff rows and implementation closure briefs, then plan a separate efficiency-foundation sprint and a later status/graph/diff-table UI sprint.

accepted_decisions.summary: Feedback builds must persist approved diff-table rows as the user-facing intent contract before documentation or implementation work proceeds.
Implementation builds must include a closure brief for user trust: intent, layer changes, acceptance evidence, checks, non-goals preserved, and remaining risks.
Builds should become high-signal, token-efficient transition points with explicit consumes/produces links and lint/schema enforcement.
Roadmap should include a Build Contract v2 sprint now, an Efficiency Foundation sprint next, and a later Status/Graph/Diff Table UI sprint.
Diff tables should become a first-class tool/UI surface eventually: pending rows live in runtime/session state, accepted rows compile into feedback builds.

assumptions: Current codewiki_build schema cannot yet accept diff_table or closure_brief fields; the first implementation task will add those fields and enforcement.
Sprint entities are not implemented yet, so initial roadmap entries can use tasks/labels to represent sprint cohorts until the sprint model lands.
open_questions: Confirm Pi session spawning API before implementing automatic session-per-sprint parallelism.
non_goals: Do not build UI before the data contract exists.
Do not migrate every historical build immediately; treat historical builds as cold legacy unless they are active.
Do not make pending diff tables canonical truth before user acceptance.

knowledge: Document Build Contract v2, mandatory feedback diff rows, mandatory implementation closure brief, roadmap sprint cohorts, agency scope/budgets, and future diff-table UI.
roadmap: Create tasks for Build Contract v2, Efficiency Foundation, and Status/Graph/Diff Table UI.
code: Later implementation will update build schemas/writers/lint/graph/API and eventually UI.

diff_table.current_state: Feedback skill can show diff tables, but feedback_build artifacts cannot persist approved diff rows.
diff_table.desired_state: feedback_build v2 requires approved diff_table rows as the user-facing intent contract.
diff_table.rationale: User trust depends on seeing that accepted intent survived into canonical handoff truth.

diff_table.current_state: Implementation builds capture evidence but lack a concise user-facing closure brief.
diff_table.desired_state: implementation_build v2 requires closure_brief with intent, layer changes, acceptance evidence, checks, preserved non-goals, and remaining risks.
diff_table.rationale: Users need compact proof that intent was implemented through knowledge, roadmap, code, checks, and validation.

diff_table.current_state: Build consumption and outputs are partly inferred from legacy fields.
diff_table.desired_state: Builds expose explicit consumes and produces edges for graph reconciliation and hot/cold lifecycle management.
diff_table.rationale: Explicit build DAG edges reduce drift ambiguity and token-heavy audits.

diff_table.current_state: Roadmap has tasks but no active sprint/cohort model for scoped agency and parallelism.
diff_table.desired_state: Plan an Efficiency Foundation sprint with config schema v4, sprint model, workflow cursor, build DAG, and GC/checkpoint policy.
diff_table.rationale: Scoped hot state lets agency run per roadmap, sprint, or task within token/time/cost budgets.

diff_table.current_state: Diff tables are chat-only and not interactive.
diff_table.desired_state: Plan a later UI sprint for status panel, graph visualization, and interactive diff-table approval/edit/reject/defer flows.
diff_table.rationale: UI should sit on top of stable contracts, not invent hidden truth.
